cards.title: Nvidia CEO Eyes $200B AI Agent CPU Market
cards.body: Jensen Huang predicts CPUs for AI agents represent the next major opportunity for Nvidia. The CEO believes this market could be worth $200 billion.

cards.title: Anthropic Targets First Profitable Quarter
cards.body: Anthropic told investors it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in Q2. The milestone marks a path toward profitability for the AI company.

cards.title: Video Clipping Startup Clouted Raises $7M
cards.body: Clouted aims to remove guesswork from making viral short videos. The startup secured a seed round led by Slow Ventures.

cards.title: xAI Lost $6.4B in 2025, Plans Major Expansion
cards.body: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals xAI's $6.4 billion loss last year while planning aggressive Grok expansion. The filing offers first public details of Elon Musk's AI financials and ambitions.

cards.title: Nvidia Posts Record Quarter, Holds $43B Startup Stakes
cards.body: Nvidia announced record revenue but forecasted slower growth in the following quarter. The company revealed $43 billion in holdings across startup investments.

cards.title: xAI Plans $2.8B Purchase of Gas Turbines
cards.body: Elon Musk's xAI will buy $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over three years. The investment supports massive data center infrastructure expansion.

cards.title: Anthropic Will Pay xAI $1.25B Monthly
cards.body: Anthropic struck a compute deal with Elon Musk's xAI valued at $1.25 billion per month. The arrangement represents a major partnership in the AI industry.

cards.title: OpenAI Solves 80-Year-Old Math Problem
cards.body: OpenAI's reasoning model disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946. Mathematicians who previously exposed OpenAI's claims are backing this breakthrough.

cards.title: IrisGo Launches AI Desktop Assistant, Backed by Andrew Ng
cards.body: Iris watches desktop activity and automatically learns to complete user tasks. The AI butler startup is backed by prominent AI researcher Andrew Ng.

cards.title: OpenAI Eyes September IPO After Legal Victory
cards.body: A day after Elon Musk's lawsuit was rejected, OpenAI resumed IPO preparations. The company aims to go public as early as September.

cards.title: AI Search Startups Emerge as Hottest Sector
cards.body: AI search has quietly become one of the most attractive targets in consumer AI. Multiple startups are racing to challenge traditional search incumbents.

cards.title: Stability AI Releases Audio Model 3.0
cards.body: Stability Audio 3.0 can generate six-minute songs and run on-device. The small model produces two-minute tracks with improved quality.

cards.title: Startup Battlefield 200 Applications Close May 27
cards.body: Applications for TechCrunch Disrupt's Startup Battlefield 200 close in one week. Winners gain investor access, $100K prize, and scaling perks.

cards.title: NanoClaw Rejects $20M Buyout, Raises $12M Seed
cards.body: NanoClaw creator turned down a $20 million acquisition offer instead. The AI agent startup raised $12 million seed funding and runs sandboxed in containers.

cards.title: Figma Adds AI Assistant to Canvas
cards.body: Figma's new AI agent accepts natural language prompts to generate and edit designs. Users can automate iterations and design tasks directly.

cards.title: Google Declares AI Design Dominance at IO 2026
cards.body: Google showcased new AI design capabilities targeting broad audiences. The company positioned itself as a contender in AI-powered design tools.

cards.title: Gmail Gets Conversational Voice Search via Gemini
cards.body: Google expanded Gmail's AI Inbox with voice-powered searches. Users can now ask Gemini to find specific email details conversationally.

cards.title: Google Launches AI Information Agents
cards.body: Google's new agentic information agents monitor topics and proactively alert users. The AI agents go beyond standard search to predict user needs.

cards.title: Ocean Raises $28M to Fight AI Phishing
cards.body: Ocean's agentic email security platform uses AI to analyze context for fraud detection. The startup claims it can identify impersonation and phishing attempts.

cards.title: Musk's OpenAI Lawsuit Rejected by Jury
cards.body: A jury swiftly rejected Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI founders and Microsoft. The decision reflected weak legal grounds partly due to Musk's delayed filing.

cards.title: Jensen Huang Eyes $200B AI Agent CPU Market
cards.body: Nvidia's CEO predicts the next major opportunity for the company will be CPUs designed for AI agents, worth approximately $200 billion. This represents Huang's vision for the next growth frontier beyond current GPU dominance.

cards.title: Anthropic Approaching First Profitable Quarter
cards.body: Anthropic has informed investors it will more than double revenue to around $10.9 billion in Q2. The AI safety company is on track to achieve profitability milestone.

cards.title: xAI Lost $6.4B in 2025, Expanding Grok
cards.body: SpaceX's IPO filing reveals Elon Musk's xAI burned through $6.4 billion last year while planning massive expansion. The filing provides rare public insight into xAI's financial losses and ambitions.

cards.title: Nvidia Records Another Record Quarter
cards.body: Nvidia announced record revenue figures but forecasted slower growth in the following quarter. The company also revealed $43 billion in startup holdings.

cards.title: xAI Buying $2.8B in Natural Gas Turbines
cards.body: Elon Musk's xAI will purchase $2.8 billion worth of natural gas turbines over three years, according to SpaceX's IPO filing. The massive infrastructure investment supports the company's data center expansion plans.

cards.title: Anthropic Pays xAI $1.25B Monthly for Compute
cards.body: Anthropic struck a deal with xAI to purchase compute resources at $1.25 billion per month. The partnership signals collaboration between major AI rivals.

cards.title: OpenAI Claims Solving 80-Year-Old Math Problem
cards.body: OpenAI's reasoning model allegedly disproved a geometry conjecture unsolved since 1946. Mathematicians who exposed OpenAI's previous false claims are backing this new one.

cards.title: Andrew Ng-Backed IrisGo AI Desktop Assistant
cards.body: IrisGo, backed by AI pioneer Andrew Ng, functions as an AI desktop buddy that watches user activity and learns to automate tasks. The startup aims to become an indispensable AI butler for computer users.

cards.title: OpenAI IPO Could Happen in September
cards.body: Following Elon Musk's lawsuit defeat, OpenAI is reportedly resuming IPO preparations. The company may go public as early as September 2026.

cards.title: AI Search Startups Attracting Major Investment
cards.body: AI search has become one of the most attractive targets in consumer AI investment. The space is experiencing significant growth and startup activity.

cards.title: Stability AI Releases Audio Model for Songs
cards.body: Stability Audio 3.0 can generate six-minute long songs with its small model running on-device. The audio AI tool produces two-minute tracks with improved quality.

cards.title: NanoClaw Creator Rejects $20M Buyout Offer
cards.body: NanoClaw creator turned down a $20 million acquisition and raised $12 million in seed funding instead. The secure AI alternative runs in sandboxed containers for agent-based marketing work.

cards.title: Figma Launches AI Assistant for Designers
cards.body: Figma added an AI agent that uses natural language prompts to generate designs and automate editing tasks. Users can now direct the assistant to create design iterations automatically.

cards.title: Google Positions Itself as AI Design Leader
cards.body: Google announced new AI design capabilities at I/O 2026, targeting accessibility for teachers and business owners. The company is declaring itself a contender in AI-powered design tools.

cards.title: Gmail Gets Conversational AI Voice Search
cards.body: Google expanded Gmail's AI Inbox with conversational voice search powered by Gemini. Users can now ask the AI to locate buried email details using natural conversation.

cards.title: Google Launches Agentic Information Agents
cards.body: Google is launching AI-powered information agents that monitor topics in the background. These agents proactively alert users to updates and changes on tracked subjects.

cards.title: Ocean Raises $28M for AI Email Security
cards.body: Ocean, an agentic email security platform, raised $28 million to fight AI-powered phishing. The startup uses AI to analyze email context and detect fraud attempts.

cards.title: Elon Musk Loses Lawsuit Against OpenAI
cards.body: A jury rejected Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI founders Sam Altman and Greg Brockman. The decision confirmed Musk's case was weak, partly due to filing delays.

cards.title: Google Preparing to Remake Search with AI
cards.body: Google is set to transform search functionality using agentic AI technology in 2026. The overhaul represents a major shift in how users will interact with search.

cards.title: Musk v. Altman Trial Concludes at OpenAI
cards.body: Elon Musk lost his lawsuit against OpenAI, with the jury finding he sued too late under statutes of limitations. The case centered on alleged deception over OpenAI's nonprofit status.

cards.title: Why Elon Musk Lost OpenAI Lawsuit
cards.body: The jury unanimously voted that Musk sued OpenAI too late, barring his claims. US District Judge Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers immediately accepted the advisory verdict.

cards.title: Google Declares Third Place in AI Models
cards.body: At Google I/O, the company will position itself as the clear third contender in the foundation model race. Google aims to demonstrate competitive AI capabilities against OpenAI and Anthropic.
